metadata.title: Changes of people’s fertility attitudes based on analysis of online public opinion on three-child policy in China

abstract: In response to the population aging, on May 31, 2021, the Political Bureau of the Central Committee of the Communist Party of China (CPC) proposed the policy that a couple can have three children and rolled out more supportive measures to further optimize fertility policies, which is another major initiative following the universal two-child policy introduced in November 2015. Currently, a series of population policy innovations have aroused great attractions among the public and triggered a hot debating on the Internet. People's fertility attitude tendency under different related policies can reflect their current fertility intentions. Based on the fact, this paper firstly classifies the sentiment of online comment data on the three-child policy and analyzes people's sentiment tendency toward the three-child policy from the spatio-temporal perspectives. Secondly, people's points of view on the three-child policy are summarized by using Latent Dirichlet Allocation (LDA) thematic clustering. The reasons for the change in people's fertility attitude tendency under different fertility policies are analyzed by comparing the change in people's fertility attitude tendency with the change in people's attentions. Finally, a multiple regression equation is constructed to analyze the key factors influencing people's intention to have three children by using public opinion data and its extension data. The findings demonstrate: (1) people's fertility attitudes toward the three-child policy are negative and similar among different regions; (2) compared to the two-child policy, the percentage of negative and neutral attitudes toward the three-child policy increases, while the percentage of positive attitudes decreases; (3) the increase in fertility costs, the deterioration of women's employment environment, and the change in the concept of marriage and childbirth become important reasons for the negative change in people's fertility attitudes toward different policies. Therefore, the government should take measures to reduce the burden of childbirth and guide the correct concept of marriage and childbirth to improve people's fertility intentions.

sections.header_raw: Introduction
sections.header_norm: introduction
paragraphs: The family planning policy, implemented in the 1980s, was one of China's most influential policies. In the early stage of implementation, the family planning policy was able to control the population growth rate and effectively relieved the pressure of population on economy, society and resources (Starbird et al. 2016). However, with the policy implemented in our country for more than 40 years, population aging, sex ratio imbalance at birth and other related issues came out. To handle these problems, China has made a series of adjustments to its fertility policies.

On November 15, 2013, the Chinese Government released the Resolutions on Deepening the Reforms on Several Major Issues which was the governmental attempt to further relax the One-Child Policy. According to the Resolutions, the family planning as a fundamental national population policy stayed unchanged, while the One-Child Policy was relaxed to allow couples with one partner being a solo child to be eligible to have a second child (the policy is called the Separate Two-Child Policy thereafter). This policy came into effect on January 1, 2014. The population who were allowed to have more than one child had expanded from couples who were both only children to couples with one partner as a solo child.

After two years' implementation of the Separate Two-Child Policy, the Chinese Government decided to introduce more radical reform on the family planning policy. On October 29, 2015, the Chinese Government issued a Universal Two-Child Policy in Communiqué of the Fifth Plenary Session of the 18th CPC Central Committee. Still insisting that family planning should be fundamental national policy, the new policy is a dramatic change to the long standing One-Child Policy. This Universal Two-Child Policy came into practice since January 1, 2016.

However, after the implementation of the two-child policy, China's birth rate did not rebound as strongly as expected. Data from the seventh population census shows that China's total fertility rate in 2020 was only 1.3, far below the world-accepted fertility replacement level. To further curb the downward trend of the birth rate, on May 31, 2021, based on the Decision on Optimizing Fertility Policy for Long-term Balanced Population Development, the Political Bureau of the CPC Central Committee proposed to further optimize the fertility policy and implement the policy that a couple can have three children and more supportive measures.

In fact, from the "separate two-child" policy to the "universal two-child" policy, to the "three-child policy" announced in May 2021, changes in China's fertility policy have attracted a great deal of public attention and sparked discussions on social media platforms about the policies. On the night that Xinhua News Agency announced the official adjustment of the population policy to allow a couple to have three children, topics such as "#three-child fertility policy is launched" and "#how to increase the willingness to have three children" rushed to the top of the search, and the number of visits regarding the related topics also rose rapidly. The comments of netizen on the three-child policy posts contain their jokes and real thoughts on fertility issues. Indepth analysis of relevant comments by netizens can not only obtain more widely distributed and abundant public opinions, but also refine the livelihood needs behind them, so as to provide the most authentic and effective public feedback for the implementation of the three-child policy and provide scientific basis for the adjustment of the fertility policy. In addition, the government has continuously introduced fertility support measures to improve people's fertility willingness. By comparing the fertility attitudes of netizens under different fertility policies, the influencing factors of netizens' fertility attitudes can be effectively analyzed, and reasonable suggestions can be provided for the government to introduce subsequent fertility policies and measures. For instance, Li, Li, and Li (2021) analyzed the trend of public opinion on the three-child policy and corresponding supporting measures by taking the comments on eight official Weibo posts as the research object. The results showed that netizens generally had negative and neutral feelings on the three-child policy and supporting measures.

At present, there are relatively few studies on the three-child policy, and there are limitations in the research content and research methods. In terms of research objectives, most of the current studies focus on investigating people's fertility intentions and their influencing factors in the context of the three-child policy. For example, Zhang et al. (2022) investigated the fertility intention and its influencing factors of young people aged 18-28 years after the release of the three-child policy. The results had shown that childbirth anxiety, parenthood anxiety and other childbearing anxiety were negatively correlated with the fertility intention of young people. In addition, from the perspective of research methods, these studies mostly use questionnaires to conduct surveys, which were constrained with time lag. For instance, Jing et al. (2022) conducted a nationwide survey on the intention to give birth again, and the results found that the barriers to child-rearing were the main reason why people were reluctant to give birth again. However, few scholars have conducted in-depth analysis of online public opinion after the release of birth policies and public attitudes after the release of different birth policies. By studying the online comments of netizens to refine the needs of people's livelihood behind them, we are able to obtain more widely distributed and richer public opinions, so as to provide the most real and effective public feedback for the implementation of the three-child policy, provide a scientific basis for the adjustment of the birth policy, and make up for the lag and high economic cost of traditional questionnaire methods. This paper uses data mining to obtain a large number of public online comments, on the one hand to make up for the shortcomings of the traditional questionnaire method of lag and high economic cost; On the other hand, by comparing people's fertility attitudes under different fertility policies, the factors affecting people's fertility intention are explored, and the research gap between online public opinion, fertility policy and fertility intention is also filled.

As such, this paper aims to study online public opinion after the release of the three-child policy. Through in-depth mining and analysis of online comment data of the three-child policy, it extracts and compares people's attitudes and opinions on different fertility policies from online comments, and establishes a regression model based on the online comment information to explore the influencing factors of people's fertility attitudes. In order to understand the real needs of the public for childbirth, we also put forward suggestions for the government to improve the willingness of the public to give birth, and beef up the enthusiasm of the public to solve the problem of difficult fertility.

The rest of the paper is arranged as follows: Section 2 is the literature review. Section 3 is the research framework of this paper. Section 4 is the description of the data sources and processing methods of this paper. Section 5 analyzes people's emotional tendency toward the three-child policy and their attention content based on online comments. Section 6 explores the changes in people's fertility attitude and its reasons under different fertility policies. Section 7 analyzes the factors influencing fertility intention in online public opinion. Section 8 is the discussion of the research results of this paper. Section 9 concludes the whole paper as well as the prospects for future work.

sections.header_raw: The Impact of the Implementation of Fertility Policy
sections.header_norm: the impact of the implementation of fertility policy
paragraphs: Current scholars have analyzed the impact of the implementation of fertility policies at both micro and macro levels. At the micro level, current research has mainly explored the impact of fertility policies on household consumption and savings. For example, Tan and Liu (2020) made it clear through mechanism analysis that the two-child policy affects family savings by increasing expenditure and temporarily reducing family income. Jia, Zhou, and Yang (2021) studied the intergenerational resource allocation of urban families in China based on the data from the China Family Financial Survey and found that after the implementation of the separate two-child policy, there was no intergenerational competition between children and the elderly in the family. At the same time, the heterogeneity of policy effects showed that the reduction of savings rate was more. Na and Kim (2022) found that the two-child policy promoted the upgrading of family consumption level and consumption structure based on the panel data of China Family Tracking Survey Database from 2012 to 2018. Meanwhile, the study found the heterogeneity of the mechanism of the two-child policy through triple difference method, i.e., the higher the wealth level of the household was, the more the fertility policy promoted an increase in household consumption and an upgrade of the consumption structure.

In addition, at the macro level, the existing literature mainly explores the effects of fertility policies on economic development, social public services, and on China's population. The typical literature on the impact of fertility policy on economic development is as follows: Liao (2013) discussed the impact of the one-child policy through a three-generation overlapping model with fertility constraints, and the results showed that the one-child policy promoted human capital accumulation and increased per capita output. Huang et al. (2019) discussed the impact of fertility policy changes on China's population and economy by combining five shared socioeconomic paths and three fertility policies. Liu (2020) integrated the two-child policy into a complex model characterized by population, resources and economic growth to explore the promoting effect of the two-child policy on the economy. The simulation results showed that the economic growth brought by the two-child policy was limited, and the combination of the two-child policy and the policy to promote the growth of human capital was economically more beneficial.

Moreover, the typical literature on the impact of fertility policy on social public services is as follows: Zhao et al. (2019) quantitatively assessed the pressure of the twochild policy on basic public health services (BPHS) by comparing workload changes in maternal and child health management and childhood immunization. Zeng, Zhang, and Liu (2017) established an actuarial model to analyze the impact of the birth policy adjustment on the pension fund and found that after the implementation of the universal two-child policy, the accumulated deficit of the pension fund would be delayed under different birth intentions. Xie et al. (2020) discussed the impact of China's birth policy changing from separate two-child policy to universal two-child policy on the balance of urban employees' basic medical insurance fund. The study found that the change of birth policy would lead to the reduction of the deficit of the balance of urban employees' basic medical insurance fund. Asante et al. (2021) explored the impact of the universal two-child policy on the maternity insurance fund in Jiangsu Province and Guangxi Zhuang Autonomous Region based on the data from 2011 to 2019, and found that the impact of the universal two-child policy on the maternity insurance fund declined over time. Zhang, Huang, and Luo (2021) constructed a dynamic actuarial model of medical insurance fund and studied and analyzed the impact of the adjustment of fertility policy and the possible improvement of fertility intention on the sustainability of the insured population and medical insurance fund.

The typical literature on the impact of fertility policy on China's population structure is as follows: Liu and Liu (2018) explored the effectiveness of China's fertility policy and factors affecting fertility choice by constructing a three-cycle overlapping generation model. The results showed that there was a significant U-shaped relationship between female income and the choice of having two children, and even if the two-child policy was further relaxed, the effect on the choice of having two children would be minor. Zhang, Guo, and Zhai (2019) established a fertility simulation model using the population census data and 1% population sampling data from 1982 to 2015 to explore the impact of the two-child policy on the total fertility rate. The results showed that the impact of the universal twochild policy on China's fertility rate was temporary. Xie (2021) used the data of Chinese General Social Survey (CGSS) in 2013 and 2015 to study the impact of China's two-child policy on the fertility rate through the Logit Model, and found that the two-child policy had not shown any improvement in the overall fertility rate of China in the short term. Li, Jiao, and Liu (2019) used the simultaneous group component method and Leslie matrix to establish a population prediction model to evaluate the impact of the implementation of the two-child policy on the size and structure of China's population. It was found that the comprehensive two-child policy would not change the trend of China's pension coefficient and aging index, but would slightly slow down its growth rate. Liu and Liu (2020) predicted the population structure of Beijing under four fertility scenarios from 2020 to 2050 based on the cohort component method, and the results showed that the basic development trend of Beijing's population would not change fundamentally under the two-child policy. Li, Yi, and Zhang (2011) conducted double differential tests on the 2000 and 2005 population censuses data and found that the impact of the one-child policy accounted for about 57% and 54% of the total increase in the sex ratio of the birth cohorts from 1991 to 2000 and from 2001 to 2005, respectively. Stuart et al. (2019) used the comprehensive control method to evaluate the impact of the family planning policy in 1973 and 1979, and verified that the fertility rate had not increased significantly after the relaxation of the family planning policy in 2015. Fan et al. (2020) discussed the change trend of sex ratio at birth and the influencing factors of sex ratio at birth in three stages of the gradual implementation of two-child policy through the data of maternal risk monitoring system in Hebei Province. The study found that with the gradual liberalization of China's two-child policy, the sex ratio at birth in Hebei Province had dropped to a normal level, and cesarean section and maternal age were the main factors affecting the sex ratio at birth.

All in all, most of the current studies on the impact of fertility policy focus on its impacts on social and economic life, and most of these studies use census data or social questionnaire data, which lead to the lagging timeliness of the findings.

Zhang, Guo, and Zhai (2019) established a fertility simulation model using the population census data and 1% population sampling data from 1982 to 2015 to explore the impact of the two-child policy on the total fertility rate. The results showed that the impact of the universal twochild policy on China's fertility rate was temporary. Xie (2021) used the data of Chinese General Social Survey (CGSS) in 2013 and 2015 to study the impact of China's two-child policy on the fertility rate through the Logit Model, and found that the two-child policy had not shown any improvement in the overall fertility rate of China in the short term. Li, Jiao, and Liu (2019) used the simultaneous group component method and Leslie matrix to establish a population prediction model to evaluate the impact of the implementation of the two-child policy on the size and structure of China's population. It was found that the comprehensive two-child policy would not change the trend of China's pension coefficient and aging index, but would slightly slow down its growth rate. Liu and Liu (2020) predicted the population structure of Beijing under four fertility scenarios from 2020 to 2050 based on the cohort component method, and the results showed that the basic development trend of Beijing's population would not change fundamentally under the two-child policy. Li, Yi, and Zhang (2011) conducted double differential tests on the 2000 and 2005 population censuses data and found that the impact of the one-child policy accounted for about 57% and 54% of the total increase in the sex ratio of the birth cohorts from 1991 to 2000 and from 2001 to 2005, respectively. Stuart et al. (2019) used the comprehensive control method to evaluate the impact of the family planning policy in 1973 and 1979, and verified that the fertility rate had not increased significantly after the relaxation of the family planning policy in 2015. Fan et al. (2020) discussed the change trend of sex ratio at birth and the influencing factors of sex ratio at birth in three stages of the gradual implementation of two-child policy through the data of maternal risk monitoring system in Hebei Province. The study found that with the gradual liberalization of China's two-child policy, the sex ratio at birth in Hebei Province had dropped to a normal level, and cesarean section and maternal age were the main factors affecting the sex ratio at birth.

All in all, most of the current studies on the impact of fertility policy focus on its impacts on social and economic life, and most of these studies use census data or social questionnaire data, which lead to the lagging timeliness of the findings.

Current scholars have analyzed the impact of the implementation of fertility policies at both micro and macro levels. At the micro level, current research has mainly explored the impact of fertility policies on household consumption and savings. For example, Tan and Liu (2020) made it clear through mechanism analysis that the two-child policy affects family savings by increasing expenditure and temporarily reducing family income. Jia, Zhou, and Yang (2021) studied the intergenerational resource allocation of urban families in China based on the data from the China Family Financial Survey and found that after the implementation of the separate two-child policy, there was no intergenerational competition between children and the elderly in the family. At the same time, the heterogeneity of policy effects showed that the reduction of savings rate was more. Na and Kim (2022) found that the two-child policy promoted the upgrading of family consumption level and consumption structure based on the panel data of China Family Tracking Survey Database from 2012 to 2018. Meanwhile, the study found the heterogeneity of the mechanism of the two-child policy through triple difference method, i.e., the higher the wealth level of the household was, the more the fertility policy promoted an increase in household consumption and an upgrade of the consumption structure.

sections.paper_id: Chen et al. - 2023 - Changes of people’s fertility attitudes based on analysis of online public opinion on three-child po
sections.header_raw: The Online Public Opinion Caused by the Release of Fertility Policies
sections.header_norm: the online public opinion caused by the release of fertility policies
paragraphs: There are relatively few studies on the online public opinion caused by the release of fertility policies. Some typical ones are as follows: Wang and Song (2019) compared the public attention intensity and public emotional tendency of the online public opinions of "separate two-child policy" and "universal two-child policy" on Weibo from the spatio-temporal perspectives, and explored the public's views on the two sets of birth policies in different periods and regions. Liu et al. (2016) conducted a critical discourse study on public opinion on news media websites after the end of the one-child policy in China, analyzed the three topics that public opinion paid attention to after the end of the one-child policy, and discussed its enlightenment on pre-pregnancy care. Li, Jiao, and Liu (2019) collected Weibo posts containing keywords of second child and conducted emotional analysis on them, finding out that high cost and burden became the main reason why people were reluctant to give birth. While the above literature has analyzed the emotional tendencies, attention, and content of netizen's attention after adjusting fertility policies, the changes of people's emotional tendencies and specific fertility behaviors, the reasons for these changes have not been addressed in the literature. Based on the CRF (Conditional Random Field) method, Meng et al. (2022) extracted the keywords of the public opinion of the threechild policy and formed word clouds, and conducted research on the public opinion texts of the three-child policy in different provinces, analyzed the differences in the attention of different provinces to fertility, and found that the differences were related to the political and economic differences between different provinces. Rong (2021) analyzed the mechanism behind public opinion through the interactive ritual chain theory, and used data collection methods to gain insight into what the public really cares about the three-child policy. In addition, based on the micro-public opinion system, Lin and Luo (2016) interpreted the public opinion of the universal two-child policy by using macro big data analysis and micro qualitative analysis of representative views. They found that the policy could boost housing prices to a certain extent, so that some relevant policies were adjusted or canceled. In the long run, it would have a positive effect on solving the problem of singles and the aging of the population, but it would not cause the excessive expansion of China's population. Wen and Zhang (2023) built a multi-attribute semantic analysis model for policy topics based on the perspective of communication sociology, mined the three-child topic semantics from multiple attributes of topic, structure and content, and found that negative emotions toward childbearing were particularly obvious from the perspective of women and young people: women still had problems such as fertility risks, work-family imbalance, workplace discrimination, etc. The younger generation faced a constant pressure to support the elderly, get involved in employment, and play to death.

In a word, although a lot of work has been done on the fertility policy, most scholars explore the impact of the policy based on the changes of various economic and social indicators after the birth policy. Meanwhile, in the research of public opinion on the fertility policy, scholars basically explore the public opinion after the release of a birth policy. Few scholars have explored the changes in public opinion and people's fertility intention after the release of different fertility policies. Therefore, this paper takes the public comments on Weibo after the release of the "three-child policy" as the research topic. By mining netizens' comments, this paper analyzes people's emotional changes toward the fertility policy, compares people's attitudes toward fertility after the introduction of different fertility policies, analyzes the factors affecting people's attitudes toward fertility, and tries to understand the changes in people's motivation and willingness to have children and their causes. On the one hand, the research outcomes will help improve the supporting measures for childbirth to be closer to the needs of the public. On the other hand, the contributions will enrich the academic community's systematic cognition of the relationship between network public opinion and the public's fertility intention.

sections.paper_id: Chen et al. - 2023 - Changes of people’s fertility attitudes based on analysis of online public opinion on three-child po
sections.header_raw: Research Framework
sections.header_norm: research framework
paragraphs: In this paper, netizen comments on Weibo under the topic of "three-child" were collected by crawler program. After text cleaning, word segmentation and word removal of the collected microblog comments, text sentiment analysis and LDA topic clustering methods were first adopted to summarize people's attitudes and concerns about the three-child policy. Secondly, based on the previous scholars' research results, this paper compares people's fertility attitudes and concerns under different fertility policies, and analyzes the reasons for their changes. Thirdly, this article constructs multiple regression equations to analyze the specific factors that affect people's fertility attitude. Finally, the above results are summarized, and the countermeasures and suggestions to improve the fertility trend are proposed. In addition, since the analysis of public opinion on fertility policy can only judge individual attitudes toward fertility from each comment, the information obtained is more of a tendency judgment (Li, Jiao, and Liu 2019). In this paper, people's emotional tendency under the comment of fertility policy is expressed as the tendency of public fertility attitude, which reflects the individual's tendency for or against the behavior of fertility intention reflected in the comment. The research framework of this paper is shown in Figure 1.

sections.paper_id: Chen et al. - 2023 - Changes of people’s fertility attitudes based on analysis of online public opinion on three-child po
sections.header_raw: Data Collection
sections.header_norm: data collection
sections.container_raw: Data and Methods
sections.container_norm: data and methods
paragraphs: With the development of social networks, Internet users are increasingly using social media services to share their views and experiences about specific products, policies, and events ( Asghar et al. 2017).Online discussion platforms in China are an important source for understanding dynamics of public opinions on specific issues and policies (Medaglia and Yang 2017). Social media provides not only a rich data source but also a cheap and efficient way to monitor and assess pubic opinions (Jiang, Qiang, and Lin 2016). In addition, social data can help policy-makers to rate available policy options based on citizens' preferences during the public policy making phase (Ceron and Negri 2016). Therefore, it is common to study the effects of policy implementation through online public opinion.

As one of the most important and popular social media in China, Weibo has similar functions to Twitter, Facebook and other social media, and netizens from all over the world can register as its users, post their attention to a tweet through re-tweets, comments, likes, etc., and interact with communicators in this way. Weibo comments reflect users' attitudes and opinions on public events, which are of great research significance and reference value, and the analysis of emotional tendencies of their contents can show the public opinion trends of the studied events (Cao 2018). In addition, since Weibo users are also the recipients and disseminators of information, their participation makes the content of Weibo comments have extensive viewpoints and clear direction, so Weibo comments can influence the direction of public opinion more than the Weibo topics themselves (Chen and Ju 2020). In this sense, Weibo serves as a window to share public opinion.

Based on this, the expressions "three-child policy is adopted," "supportive measures for three-child," "how to increase the willingness of three-child" as well as other hot topics on Weibo are selected as keywords. The article uses a crawler to capture 75,781 comments on Weibo, which includes: commenter ID, commenter gender, commenter address, comment date, comment content, comment like count, comment reply count. Finally, after cleaning the invalid comments and duplicate comments, a total of 64,929 comments about the threechild birth policy and related supportive measures are adopted. The specific distribution of comments is shown in Figure 2, which demonstrates that from various provinces in China, the distribution differs widely, among which, Beijing, Shanghai, Shandong and other provinces and cities have a large number of comments, while Gansu, Tibet and other provinces have a smaller number of comments.

paragraphs: Text pre-processing is important to affect the accuracy of its mining results, which includes the process of structuring the original text, screening effective information, forming structured data through word segmentation, and removing stop words, as shown below.

(1) Jieba word segmentation Jieba word segmentation is a Python package for Chinese word splitting. Based on the directed acyclic graph finding algorithm, through dynamic planning, it maximizes the probability of the union of the word cut combinations. Jieba word segmentation can split Chinese text, annotate lexical categories, extract keyword, and add stop word dictionaries and custom dictionaries, which is a commonly used word segmentation tool at present. In this paper, Jieba word segmentation library is adopted to segment comment text, which can reflect the custom dictionaries of fertility policy-related content. However, the initial word segmentation results are poorly applied, and some proper nouns do not reflect proprietary attributes, e.g., "one child" is cut into "one child" and "child," and "three-child policy" is cut into "three-child" and "policy." Therefore, to avoid inaccurate word cutting in policy documents, this paper applies custom dictionaries in the original dictionaries through a large number of repeated experiments, which greatly improves the accuracy of document topic mining and makes the topic-matching analysis more referential.

(2) Remove stop words Stop words refer to words or phrases that are automatically filtered out when processing text during natural language process to improve the quality of text features or reduce dimension of text features. Generally speaking, in addition to the accuracy of word segmentation, there are auxiliary verbs and tone auxiliaries appeared frequently, which reduce the quality of text features. The construction of a stop word list significantly impacts the effect of word segmentation. Therefore, to improve the quality of word features in the text of fertility policy, this paper constructs a stop word list. Based on the available resources on the Internet, the Stop Word List of Harbin Institute of Technology, the Stop Word List of Machine Learning Intelligence Laboratory of Sichuan University, the Stop Word List of Baidu, the initial stop word list is obtained by text filtering and cleaning, extracting Chinese words from various stop word lists.

In addition to the above stop words summarized from online resources, there are some stop words specific to the policy text that need to be deleted, such as "opinion," "State Council," and "government." These words appear frequently but do not play a key role in determining the topic, which should be deleted from the stop word list of the three-child birth policy in this paper.

paragraphs: Sentiment analysis, also known as tendency analysis, refers to the extraction and classification of the sentiment tendencies embedded in subjective texts (Liu 2011). Weibo comment text sentiment analysis is to analyze the sentiment tendencies of subjective remarks made by netizens on Weibo, which belongs to a field of text sentiment analysis. In this paper, SnowNlp sentiment analysis model, a class library in Python, is adopted to perform sentiment analysis on user review data.

SnowNlp's sentiment analysis is based on the principle of Naive Bayes model. Due to the classification of n categories c 1; . . . ; c nwith featuresw 1; . . . ; w n, the basic process of category c j is as follows:

SnowNlp calculates the prior probability based on the labeled training set, simplifies the pre-classified dataset using the built-in word segmentation algorithm and removing stop words, and calculates the occurrence probability of each feature and the occurrence probability of different features, and finally calculates the posterior probability that the feature belongs to category. If P c j jw 1 ; . . . ; w n À � ¼ max P c 1 jw 1 ; . . . ; w n ð Þ; . . . ; P c m jw 1 ; . . . ; w n ð Þ f g, under the condition w 1 ; . . . ; w n , this category belongs toc j . In this section, categoryc j belongs to positive or negative emotion category. w 1 ; . . . ; w n is word embedding, P c j jw 1 ; . . . ; w n À � is the emotion value of each comment.

The process of SnowNlp sentiment analysis is shown in Figure 3. In addition, in terms of word segmentation, since SnowNlp is unable to identify negative words when segmenting words and Jieba word segmentation is relatively better (Zeng et al. 2020), Jieba word segmentation is chosen instead of SnowNlp's own segmentation words in this paper. In addition, the nouns specific to the three-child birth policy are added to Jieba's custom corpus here, as described in Section 4.2.1, thus improving the accuracy of sentiment judgment. Meanwhile, when dealing with stop words, a customized stop word corpus is used in this paper to improve judgment accuracy. It should be noted that due to limitations and shortages that SnowNlp corpus contains comments on different kinds of products, the sentiment prediction accuracy will be greatly limited. Therefore, it is not suitable for the sentiment analysis prediction of this paper for the comments on the three-child birth policy.

The training set is replaced with a dataset of 120,000 Weibo comments labeled with sentiment polarity, and the Bayesian classifier of SnowNlp is used to generate a model suitable for Weibo comments for more accurate sentiment judgment.

Finally, the sentiment analysis results derived from SnowNlp take values between [0, 1]. The value is closer to 0, indicating the negative sentiment is stronger, and vice versa. Besides positive and negative texts, some texts do not reflect sentiment attitudes toward the threechild policy and its related policies. Therefore, texts with values between [0, 0.4) are considered as negative emotions, values between [0.4, 0.7) are neutral, and values between [0.7, 1] are positive.

Figure 4 shows the fertility attitudes distributions of Weibo comments on the three-child policy from May 31, 2021, to April 8, 2022, according to SnowNlp's sentiment classification results. Among them, more than half (52.26%) of the comments have a negative attitude toward the implementation of the three-child policy; 34.79% of the comments have a neutral attitude toward the three-child policy, while 12.95% are positive. It indicates that the majority of people still are questionable and negative toward the fertility policy and its supportive measures.

paragraphs: The LDA model is used for extracting topics of text contents. It contains three structural elements of word, topic, and document. When a large number of similar documents need to be comparatively analyzed, the LDA model can be applied to analogize the articles by different probabilities of occurrence of a document, topic, and vocabulary. Suppose K topics and N words in M documents are analyzed, the parameter t can be used to represent the topic, the parameter w represents a single word, w m;n represents the n th word in the m th document, z m;n represents the topic of the n th word in the m th document, the parameter P t i ¼ j ð Þ represents the probability of extracting words belonging to topic j from the document, and the parameter P w i t i ¼ j ð Þ represents the probability of extracting the word belonging to topic j when the word is i. According to the above parameter settings, the probability of obtaining the i th word in the document can be calculated according to the following formula.
In this paper, Python is used to build a LDA topic model for text clustering analysis, and the specific steps are as follows.
(1) Building a corpus based on the obtained segmented text.
(2) Constructing a corpus based on the established lexicon.
(3) Generating topics and calculating coherence scores based on the models.LdaModel() function. (4) Selecting the optimal number of topics and outputting topics and keywords.
(5) Analyzing based on the output topics and corresponding keywords.

In step (3), this paper determines the optimal number of topics in each category of comments by measuring the semantic similarity among words with high scores in the topic, i.e., the optimal number of topics in each category of comments. This is because topic coherence not only provides semantic information compared to other judgment criteria but also is highly relevant to human judgment (Mimno et al. 2011).

To extract more valid information from the comments, the coherence scores of the three categories of fertility attitudes comment texts obtained in Section 4.2 are calculated, and the topic number optimization is performed to obtain the variations of coherence score for the three categories as shown in Figures 567.
According to its coherence score change curve with the number of topics, the number K of positive comments, neutral comments, and negative comments is set as follows: 1, 2, and 4.

paragraphs: Figure 8 shows the distribution of five emotional tendencies in online comments. Among them, the people who oppose the three-child policy are the most, accounting for 45.06%, while the people who strongly support and strongly oppose the three-child policy account for 5.33% and 7.2%, respectively. By comparing the results of the three categories of emotion in Section 4.3, it can be seen that most netizens maintain a general opposition and neutral attitude toward the three-child policy, while few netizens show strong opposition or support to the three-child policy. This shows that most people have a relatively flat attitude toward the three-child policy, although there is resistance, but the resistance is not strong. Therefore, it is necessary for the government to understand the causes of people's resistance, so as to eliminate people's negative emotions toward fertility, and thus achieve the purpose of raising the fertility rate.

paragraphs: Figure 9 shows the sentiment distributions of daily comments. According to the curves, the overall sentiment of the public is relatively stable, with positive attitudes fluctuating greatly in the early stage and decreasing significantly in the later stage. Opposed and neutral attitudes continuously fluctuate, remaining within a certain range. The reasons are as follows: (1) as the government gradually liberalizes the restrictions on childbirth, people have psychological expectations for the release of the three-child birth policy, and thus their sentiment tendency is more stable; (2) more than half of the comments on the birth policy are negative, and thus their negative attitude tendency fluctuates slightly, indicating people's fertility intention is always in a low state.

paragraphs: Based on the crawled comment IP addresses and the result of fertility attitudes classification, Figure 10 shows the sentiment distribution of comments in each province, where the horizontal axis represents the province, the vertical axis represents the sentiment distribution in each province, and the ratio of the strongly opposed, opposed, neutral, supportive, and strongly supportive sentiment of comments in the corresponding provinces to the overall comments in each province are also shown from bottom to top. According to the sentiment distribution in each province, it can be seen that the distribution of people's opinions on the three-child birth policy is relatively stable in different provinces due to people's expectations of the birth policy. After the implementation of the two-child birth policy, the government adopts a series of measures to encourage childbirth, and people also anticipate that the government will further relax birth restrictions and introduce new population policies. At the same time, due to the popularity of the Internet, some reports on the Internet about dink form a demonstration effect. When personal fertility concepts differ from those of celebrities in the media, people are more inclined to compromise and agree with the celebrities' views, and this approval effect will intensify the prevalence of low fertility intentions (Barber and Axinn 2004). Based on this, people's views on the three-child fertility policy tend to be the same in different provinces affected by the Internet.

paragraphs: In this section, the LDA topic clustering model is used to cluster the comments on various fertility attitudes. Since there are few comments that strongly oppose and strongly support the fertility policy, this section adopts the results of emotion classification in section 4.3 for cluster analysis and their optimal number of topics is determined by the coherence score, while the ten most frequently occurring words in each category of comments are selected as the main content of the people's attention in that category for analysis. Through qualitative analysis of the content of public concern, we can understand the reasons for the low fertility willingness of the public, so as to provide more effective suggestions for the introduction of fertility support measures.

Based on the results of topic number optimization in Section 4.4, the number of topics extracted under each category of sentiment, K, is set separately, and the words with the highest probability of occurrence under each topic are generated. Among them, the coherence score of the positive category comments reaches the highest when K is equal to 1, so the number of topics for the positive category comments is set to 1, and the results are summarized in Table 1. According to Table 1, it can be seen that the positive category comments mainly expresses people's support and expectation for the three-child policy and the implementation of a series of maternity supportive measures to promote employment equality between men and women and relieve the pressure of childbirth, for example, male paternity leave, concurrent maternity leave for both men and women, and the payment of maternity allowance.
The number of topics K of neutral comments is set to 2, and the results are summarized in Table 2.

According to Table 2, it can be seen that the content mainly focuses on two aspects: (1) the rights and interests of childbirth. People believe that childbirth is women's own right, and women's freedom of childbirth should be protected, which includes the opinions of family members who want to have fewer or more children should be respected. ( 2) The policy suggestions. People believe that the release of the three-child policy is to increase the fertility rate, therefore, subsidies should be intensified for the family with the first child and second child.
The number of topics K of negative comments is set to 4, and the results are summarized in Table 3.

The keywords of each category of topics under the negative comments in Table 3 are named and analyzed. (1) Childbirth cost. People believe that the current high cost and pressure of childbirth, economic pressure, and the burden of education discourage people from the three-child policy. (2) Policy implementation. People express doubts about the operability of supporting measures for childbirth, and think that the currently released proposals such as "childbirth loans," "having children before marriage," and "promoting marriage and childbirth among graduate students" are impractical and difficult to implement. (3) Employment. People are concerned about women's employment. Female netizen believe that multiple childbirths will lead to more serious gender discrimination in the workplace, and the conflict between childcare and work will also greatly impact their development in the workplace. (4) Concept of marriage and childbirth, mainly expresses the current concept of marriage and childbirth. People believe they should follow the concept of late marriage and childbearing, fewer and better births, and some even express their reluctance to marriage and childbirth.

paragraphs: This section compares the fertility tendency based on online comments under three-child policy and two-child policy, and the results are shown in Figure 9 (data source: Chinese online public opinions on the two-child policy (Wang and Song 2019)).

In the online comments on the two-child policy, most people support this policy, accounting for 72.97%, only 11.58% are neutral, and only 15.45% are negative. However, in the online comments on the three-child policy, negative comments account for 52.26%, the neutral comments rise to 34.79%, and positive comments fall to 12.95%, as shown in Figure 11. The public's emotional tendency toward the three-child policy has changed greatly. In contrast, their attitude toward the twochild policy is more positive than that toward the three-child policy, indicating that the two-child policy is more popular with the public. The reasons for the change in people's fertility attitude will be further analyzed in the next section. opinion in the context of the birth policy adjustment (Han, Ming, and He 2020)).The proportion of various topics of the two-child and three-child policy is shown in Figures 12 and 13.
According to Table 4, people's concerns have changed significantly under different fertility policies.

The comparison of people's attention contents under different fertility policies and their proportion indicates (1) the topic of fertility cost is mentioned in the comments of both the two-child policy and the three-child policy, and its proportion in the comments of the three-child policy has increased by 7.65% compared with that of the two-child policy, which means that the fertility cost has become more and more important to people. In addition, people who made such comments all showed negative tendencies toward the three-child policy, and the cost of childbirth has deepened its influence on people and thus has a greater negative impact on their fertility attitudes (2) The proportion of women's rights and interests in the comments on the three-child policy also keeps rising, and it is derived from one topic in the comments on the two-child policy to fertility rights and interests as well as employment rights, and people in both topics show neutral or negative fertility attitudes, which indicates that women's self-awareness has become stronger, and when there is a conflict between fertility and career development, they more often choose selfdevelopment. It also reflects that women are still subject to gender discrimination in employment because of their fertility, which leads to a decrease in their fertility intention and thus negatively impacts their fertility attitudes (3) The proportion of the topic of marriage and childbearing attitudes increased by 2.76% compared with the topic of fertility intentions under the comments of the two-child policy, which mainly expresses people's recognition of modern marriage and childbearing attitudes such as "fewer and better births" and "late marriage and childbearing." At the same time, with the change in the concept of marriage and childbirth, people's desired number of children is also focused on the combination of one son and one daughter (Zhen and An 2021), and the number of children encouraged by the three-child birth policy is not consistent with their desired number of children, which has a negative impact on the fertility attitude tendency (4) Compared with the comments on the two-child policy, the comments on the three-child policy have raised doubts about a series of supportive measures for childbirth, because the three-child policy has just been introduced and supportive measures for childbirth have not yet been completed, such that "childbirth loans," "single childbirth," "early marriage age" and other related proposals are not consistent with the current psychological expectations of the public, who think that these measures are not in line with the actual social development and are difficult to implement, which causes the public to question the rationality of these policies and thus show negative tendencies in the comments. In addition, the topics of "raising a second child" and "fertility health" are no longer mentioned in the comments on the third child policy, because people's expectation of childbirth has decreased and they are no longer concerned about child rearing.

paragraphs: (1) Distribution of online public opinion and emotional attitude Through the emotional analysis of online comments on the three-child policy, it can be seen that most people are opposed to the three-child policy, which is consistent with the analysis results of comments on official Weibo by Li, Li, and Li (2021) through NLP technology. In addition, considering the complexity of reproductive behavior itself, this paper divides the public's attitude tendency into five categories according to the comment emotion score. It can be seen from the research results that few people strongly oppose the fertility policy. Therefore, it is necessary for the government to understand the reasons for the low fertility willingness of the people, so as to introduce a series of targeted fertility support measures to improve the fertility willingness of the people.

(2) Time distribution of online public opinion According to the distribution of people's fertility attitudes over time, it can be seen that people's emotional tendency to the three-child policy has a small change range, especially those who are generally opposed to it. This is inconsistent with Wang, Nie, and Liu (2021) research on the two-child policy. This is because there is a certain time between the release of the two-child policy and the three-child policy. After a series of birth policies have been released, people have already had certain psychological expectations for the release of the three-child policy. In addition, the declining desire to have children has also become an important reason.

(3) Distribution of online public opinion by province Through the spatial distribution of online public opinion, it can be seen that there are obvious differences in the attention of different provinces to the three-child policy. Meng et al. (2022) pointed out that the enthusiasm for the three-child policy was obviously different in different provinces, and the enthusiasm of provinces was related to the level of economic development. In addition, this paper also studies the variation of people's fertility attitude with regional distribution, and finds that there is little difference in people's emotional tendency toward the three-child policy in different regions.

paragraphs: This section will discuss from two aspects: people's attitudes toward fertility under different fertility policies and the changes in the content of people's online comments.

(1) Changes in online public opinion's emotional attitude tendency By comparing the fertility attitudes reflected in the online comments of the public on the comprehensive two-child policy and the three-child policy, it can be seen that the proportion of the public with a positive attitude toward the fertility policy has greatly decreased, while the proportion of the public with a negative attitude has shown an upward trend. The change of people's fertility attitude reflects people's fertility intention, which indicates that people's fertility intention shows a downward trend at present.

(2) Online public opinion concerns content changes According to the comparison of the classification results of online comments on the comprehensive two-child policy and the three-child policy, it can be seen that the birth cost has always been the topic of public concern, and its attention is on the rise. The proportion of women's rights and employment rights in the comments also show an upward trend, while people's attention to the content of the fertility policy itself show a downward trend, which indicates that under the current situation, with the implementation of the relaxed fertility policy, the direct effect of the policy on reproductive behavior has significantly weakened. The influence of social and economic development on fertility intention and fertility level is increasingly prominent (Zhuang, Jiang, and Li 2020).

paragraphs: Through the establishment of logistic regression model, this paper find that the number of comments received likes and the number of comments received replies, that is, the number of user engagement, have a significant negative impact on people's fertility attitude. This research result is consistent with the research result of Wang, Nie, and Liu (2021), that is, the higher the frequency of Internet use, the more obvious the inhibitory effect on people's fertility intention. In addition, Liu et al. (2021) also pointed out that the traditional concept of gender division of labor in China even imprisoned women in the family, and the family was responsible for family production and child rearing. However, the popularity/application of the Internet has brought about changes in women's values, and the more frequently women use the Internet, the more susceptible they are to the influence of online ideological trends, thus reducing their willingness to have children. Therefore, the government should take the negative impact of individual Internet use on fertility intention as an important factor to evaluate and predict the effect of fertility policy adjustment.

paragraphs: (1) People's sentiment toward the three-child policy is negative and has minor differences over geographical regions.

By classifying the sentiment of online comments under the three-child policy, it is clear that people's sentiment toward the three-child policy is relatively stable and negative. This indicates that people's sentiment is stable because they expect the government to introduce new population policies by gradually relaxing fertility restrictions. However, as people's fertility intention decreases, their fertility attitudes toward the three-child policy also tend to be negative. In addition, the geographical difference in people's sentiment tendency is not significant due to the popularity of the Internet, which has reduced the cost of information dissemination and increased people's recognition effect on fertility dissemination and increased people's recognition effect on fertility.

(2) People have negative attitudes toward fertility keep rising. By comparing people's fertility attitude tendencies under different fertility policies, we can see that the proportion of negative tendency increases under the three-child policy compared to the two-child policy. Further comparing and analyzing people's concerns, we can see that people's attention on realistic topics such as the fertility cost, women's rights, and the concept of marriage and childbirth increases significantly after the release of the three-child policy compared to the two-child policy. At the same time, people doubt the implementation ability of supportive measures and thus express negative attitudes. In addition, the decrease in attention to the topics of "fertility health" and "raising the second child" also indicates that people's fertility intention has decreased, and their fertility attitudes tend to be negative.

(3) People's fertility attitude tendency is negatively correlated with their participation. User activity is negatively correlated with their fertility attitude tendency, where the people's fertility attitude tendency is influenced by their participation, i.e., the higher number of likes represents the lower sentiment value. In addition, the effect of the numbers of replies on fertility attitude tendency is that the more responses people get, the lower their sentiment scores are.

paragraphs: (1) Reducing the fertility cost and alleviating the burden of childbirth According to this study, the high fertility cost is a constant concern and an important reason for people's negative attitudes toward childbirth. Therefore, to change people's fertility attitude and increase their fertility intention, the government should implement a series of measures to reduce the burden of childbirth in various aspects: 1) Reduce the housing burden of families by opening up applications for affordable housing for families that meet the conditions for childbirth as well as the financial conditions, and at the same time reduce the mortgage interest rate for families that have already had children. 2) Intensify the construction of public childcare services, provide financial subsidies to communities, and establish more childcare services in the communities to facilitate people to take care of their children nearby and lower the time and financial costs of childcare. 3) Promote the balanced development of compulsory education, allocate educational resources such as schools and teachers reasonably in each region, and at the same time, schools should take the main responsibility of education to avoid the phenomenon that parents assist teachers to grade homework, which will reduce the burden of education on parents.

(2) Guiding the concept of marriage and childbirth to reduce fertility anxiety The decreasing willingness to marry and the change in fertility concept affect people's fertility attitude. Under the context of "not getting married," efforts should be made to strengthen the management of the virtual space of the Internet, improve the social governance ability under "Internet+," optimize the network environment, reduce the spread of negative information, spread positive concepts of marriage and childbirth, advocate a harmonious family atmosphere, and establish correct values. At the same time, the correct popularization of fertility knowledge on the Internet should be advocated to help people make fertility plans and avoid the demonization of fertility, thereby reducing fertility anxiety.
